source: BillShark data, Consumer Reports surveys, FTC complaint data, community-sourced

comcast_xfinity.name: Comcast / Xfinity
tips: Call the cancellation/retention department directly — they have the best deals
Have a competitor's price ready (T-Mobile Home Internet, AT&T Fiber)
Ask for the 'new customer promotion' to be applied to your account
Best time to call: Tuesday-Thursday, 9-11am (shorter wait, reps less stressed)
If first rep says no, hang up and call again — different rep, different answer
Mention you've been a customer for X years — loyalty matters
comcast_xfinity.script: Hi, I'm considering switching my internet service because my bill has gone up to $[current]. I've been a loyal customer for [years] and I'd like to stay, but [competitor] is offering [their price] for the same speed. Is there anything you can do to keep my business?
comcast_xfinity.escalationPhrase: I understand. Could you transfer me to your retention or loyalty department? I'd like to explore my options before I cancel.

att.name: AT&T
tips: AT&T retention department is called 'Loyalty' — ask for them directly
Bundling discounts are often available if you have wireless + internet
Fiber customers have less leverage (less competition) but can still get speed upgrades
Ask about any current promotions not advertised online
att.script: Hi, I've noticed my bill has increased to $[current] per month. I've been looking at other options and I found [competitor] offering [price]. I'd prefer to stay with AT&T since I've been here [years], but I need the price to come down. What can you offer?

spectrum.name: Spectrum (Charter)
tips: Spectrum is harder to negotiate because they don't do contracts — but that also means no cancellation fee
Threaten to switch to T-Mobile Home Internet or Starlink
Ask for a 'limited time promotional rate'
If they won't budge, actually cancel — they often call back within a week with an offer
spectrum.script: I'm calling because my bill is now $[current] which is significantly more than when I signed up. T-Mobile Home Internet is offering $50/month with no price increases. Unless you can match something close to that, I'll need to switch.

tmobile_home.name: T-Mobile Home Internet
tips: T-Mobile Home Internet is already priced aggressively ($50/month) with price lock guarantee. Not much room to negotiate, but it's a great card to play against cable companies.
Switching carriers saves the most — loyalty is expensive in wireless
Check for employer discounts (many companies get 15-25% off major carriers)
MVNOs (Mint Mobile, Visible, Cricket) use the same networks for 50-70% less
Family plans are always cheaper per line — find friends to share a plan

alternatives.name: Mint Mobile
alternatives.note: Must prepay 3-12 months. Best budget option.

alternatives.name: Visible
alternatives.note: Unlimited on Verizon's network. Party Pay for cheaper.

alternatives.name: Google Fi
alternatives.note: Flexible plans. Good for low data users.

tips: Car insurance is the EASIEST money to save — just get 3 quotes
Average savings from shopping: $180/year (some save $500+)
Always ask about: multi-policy, good driver, low mileage, autopay, paid-in-full discounts
Raise deductible from $500 to $1,000 — saves 15-25% on premium
If you have an emergency fund, higher deductible is smart
Good credit = lower rates in most states
Shop every 12-18 months — loyalty is not rewarded in insurance
Bundle with auto insurance for biggest discount (15-25%)
Raise deductible to $1,000-2,500 if you have emergency fund
Ask about claims-free discount if no claims in 3+ years
Security system discount: 5-15%
Non-smoker discount: 5-15%
Review coverage annually — you may be over-insured
ALWAYS negotiate medical bills — the sticker price is rarely the real price
Ask for an itemized bill first — errors are found 30-40% of the time
Ask for the 'self-pay' or 'cash pay' discount (often 20-50% off)
Set up a payment plan — most providers offer 0% interest plans
If uninsured, ask about charity care or financial assistance programs
Compare costs on Healthcare Bluebook or Fair Health before procedures
general.script: I received a bill for $[amount] and I'd like to discuss payment options. Could you tell me if there's a self-pay discount or a financial assistance program available? I'd also like to request an itemized statement to review the charges.

tips: Call to cancel and you'll often get offered a discount to stay (30-50% off for 3-6 months)
Use annual billing — almost always 15-20% cheaper than monthly
Student discounts: Spotify ($5.99), Apple Music ($5.99), Amazon Prime ($7.49/month), YouTube Premium ($7.99)
Share family plans: Spotify Family ($16.99/6 people), YouTube Premium Family ($22.99/5 people)
Check if your library card gives free access to Kanopy, Hoopla, Libby

cancelRetentionOffers.Netflix: Rarely offers discounts. Cancel and come back — they sometimes send win-back offers.
cancelRetentionOffers.Hulu: Often offers 50% off for 3 months when you try to cancel.
cancelRetentionOffers.SiriusXM: ALWAYS negotiate. Never pay full price. Typical: $5-8/month vs $17 list.
cancelRetentionOffers.Audible: Try to cancel and they'll often offer 3 months at $7.95 or a free month.
cancelRetentionOffers.NYT / WSJ: Cancel and they typically offer 50-75% off to retain.

tips: Negotiate rent at renewal — landlords lose $3,000-5,000 on turnover
Frame it as: 'I'd like to stay, but I need the rent to stay at $[current]'
Offer to sign a longer lease (18-24 months) for a lower rate
Offer to pay several months upfront for a discount
Being a good tenant IS leverage — on-time payments, no complaints, no damage
Research comparable rents in the area to show your current rate is at or above market
Best time to negotiate: 60-90 days before lease ends
general.script: Hi [landlord], I've really enjoyed living here and I'd love to renew my lease. I noticed the proposed increase to $[new amount]. I've been researching comparable units in the area and found several at $[lower comp]. Given that I've been a reliable tenant — always on time with rent, no complaints, and I take care of the place — I'd like to propose renewing at $[target]. I'm happy to sign a longer lease if that helps.
